symptoms.symptom_name: Traumatic bleeding from an avulsed raised pigmented lesion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Friable, vascular keratotic lesions bleed briskly when the crust is avulsed — dramatic but benign.
Bleeding can occur with an ulcerated melanoma too, so bleeding alone neither confirms nor excludes malignancy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Patient-reported recent change / growth in the pigmented lesion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Change in size/colour of a pigmented lesion is a cardinal melanoma warning sign — it is what mandates in-person dermoscopy even though this lesion proves benign.
Episodic intense/blistering sun exposure is an established melanoma risk factor, raising the threshold for in-person assessment.
Absence of family history slightly lowers baseline risk but does not exclude melanoma.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dermoscopic features of a benign seborrhoeic keratosis (comedo-like openings, milia-like cysts, 'stuck-on' appearance; absent pigment network and blue-white veil)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic dermoscopic keratosis pattern — sealed in-person clinician finding; the AI Doctor cannot obtain dermoscopy and therefore cannot make this determination remotely.
Absence of melanoma dermoscopic features (pigment network, irregular globules, blue-white veil) argues strongly against melanoma — but only obtainable in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Histopathology of shave excision (absence of atypical melanocytic proliferation)

investigation_details.expected_result: Benign seborrhoeic keratosis; no atypical melanocytic proliferation
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Definitive exclusion of melanoma is deferred — the planned in-hours shave excision provides absolute confirmation.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Histopathology is the gold standard to definitively exclude melanoma — a hospital/clinic investigation never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured anxiety vitals (BP 145/92, HR 105, afebrile, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild hypertension and tachycardia with normal temperature and oxygen saturation are consistent with acute anxiety — sealed clinician measurement, not obtainable via chat and not a marker of clinical emergency.
